mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating household income and consumption capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer spending and construction investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal yields a negative Rank IC over 60-day horizons, or forward returns are indistinguishable from random walk benchmarks (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a deep recessionary regime, any month-over-month stabilization or increase in the industrial production index signals supply-side capacity utilization recovery, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods where GDPC1 contracts for two consecutive quarters, positive INDPRO changes fail to predict positive 5-day SPX returns (hit rate < 50%).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below the 12-month trailing inflation rate, real interest rates turn negative, eroding fixed-income yields and forcing equity repricing downward, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative real rate condition produces positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 4% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate wage income and corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with PAYEMS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC > -0.01).

mechanism_narrative: When real personal income growth outpaces real GDP growth, household savings rates compress and discretionary spending accelerates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The income-to-GDP growth differential fails to predict positive 20-day returns during periods of rising interest rates, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 48%.

mechanism_narrative: In a financial stress regime, simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate credit market freezing and collapsing capital expenditure, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods where credit spreads exceed 150bps, the joint decline signal yields positive 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, invalidating the credit freeze transmission channel.
